Common.freeForeverNotice: 100% Free & Open Source Forever • No Ads or Paywalls • Sustained by Divine Providence & Voluntary Donations
Common.mvpModalIntro: Welcome to Evangelizae — our 100% free, open-source Catholic digital missionary platform without ads.
Common.mvpModalPoint1Desc: The entire interface (Sanctuary, sensory Rosary with haptic vibration, Liturgy, and Intercessory Wall) is operating in a high-fidelity local demonstration mode for visual and spiritual validation.
Common.mvpModalPoint2Desc: The Theological Artificial Intelligence queries and real-time cloud database are currently operating with mock/simulated validation data. The official Magisterial RAG engine with Scripture and Catechism is under active development.

Header.drawerQuote: "Holiness consists in doing ordinary things with extraordinary love."

Footer.missionText: Evangelizae does not replace the Church, the priest, or parish life. We exist as a daily companion to lead souls to Christ, the Sacraments, and physical parish communion.
Footer.freePledge: 100% Free and Open Source Platform Forever • No Ads or Paywalls • Sustained exclusively by Providence and voluntary free-will donations from brothers and sisters in Christ.
Footer.copyright: © 2026 Evangelizae. In faithful adherence to the Magisterium of the Catholic Church.
Footer.scriptureQuote: "You received without paying; give without pay." (Matthew 10:8)

About.title: The Sacred & Doctrinal Mission of Evangelizae
About.subtitle: "Freely You Have Received; Freely Give" (Mt 10:8) — Veritas • Communio • Missio
About.introText: The modern digital world has been shaped by algorithms of distraction, anxiety, and instant vanity. Even Catholic apps and tools often fall into the trap of 'secular gamification': leaderboards, ego badges, and public rankings that turn prayer and interior life into a productivity competition.
About.introText2: Evangelizae was born as a purified refuge and a faithful missionary response aligned with the Sacred Magisterium of the Catholic Church. Our mission is not to trap your attention on a screen. For us, technology is merely a transitory tool, never an end in itself.
About.goalBoxTitle: The Ultimate Goal: From Screen to Altar
About.goalBoxQuote: "The true success of this platform happens at the exact moment you turn off the screen, kneel before the Blessed Sacrament in the Tabernacle, and live out practical charity in your physical Parish."
About.goalBoxSub: We do not want to keep you glued to your phone. We want to prepare your heart for Confession, Eucharistic Adoration, and Sacramental Life in your local community.
About.deiVerbumQuote: Sacred Tradition and Sacred Scripture form one sacred deposit of the word of God.
About.goalTagline: No anxious notifications • No digital vanity • Faithful to the Sacraments
About.providenceNote: Divine Providence & Love for the Church
About.missionValuesTitle: Our Mission, Values, and Sacred Purpose
About.missionValuesSubtitle: A 100% free, open-source apostolate dedicated to the salvation of souls and the glory of God
About.missionText: Evangelizae has as its primary purpose to sanctify the daily routine of the Catholic faithful through beauty, contemplation, and sound doctrine. We reject theological relativism and the commercialization of the sacred. Our immutable commitment is to provide free, pure digital infrastructure—without paywalls, commercial ads, or data mining—so that the Word of God and Prayer remain accessible to all.
About.activeFaithTitle: The Importance of an Active & Faithful Christian Life
About.activeFaithSubtitle: "So also faith by itself, if it does not have works, is dead" (James 2:17)
About.activeFaithText1: True spiritual life is not merely passing emotions or passive consumption of online content. Christian holiness demands daily cooperation with divine Grace: persevering mental and vocal prayer ('Pray without ceasing' — 1 Thess 5:17), daily examination of conscience, combating vice, and practicing the theological virtues (Faith, Hope, and Charity).
About.activeFaithText2: An active faith manifests in fulfilling the Commandments of God and His Church, heroic forgiveness, and concrete charity toward brethren in need. Daily devotion on Evangelizae is the spiritual fuel enabling you to be the light of the world and the salt of the earth in your family, workplace, and society.
About.churchRoleTitle: The Primordial Role of the Holy Catholic Church and the Altar
About.churchRoleSubtitle: "The Church of the living God, the pillar and bulwark of the truth" (1 Timothy 3:15)
About.churchRoleText1: The Roman Catholic Church is the Mystical Body of Christ, instituted by Our Lord upon the foundation of St. Peter (Mt 16:18). In her resides the fullness of Revealed Truth and the means of salvation. No digital platform, website, or artificial intelligence can ever substitute for our Holy Mother Church, the ordained priesthood, or the efficacy of the Seven Sacraments.
About.churchRoleText2: Evangelizae exists solely as a 'digital narthex'—a humble and transitory servant. Our ultimate objective is to lead the faithful into the physical stone temple: to the Confessional, to the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass participated with reverence, to Eucharistic Adoration (where Christ is substantially present in the Eucharist), and to fraternal communion in parish life.
About.pillarsExplanationTitle: Theological Explanation of the 4 Pillars of Our Mission
About.pillarsExplanationSubtitle: How Evangelizae harmoniously articulates the four dimensions of Catholic spirituality
About.pillar1Title: Pillar I: Prayer & Contemplation (The Daily Habit)
About.pillar1Desc: Inspired by the Method of St. Louis de Montfort ('To Jesus through Mary'). The Holy Rosary guided with sensory audio and biblical contemplation of each mystery elevates the soul to intimate union with God amidst daily responsibilities.
About.pillar2Desc: Prayerfully reading the Word (Lectio Divina) in Daily Liturgy, enriched by the wisdom of the Holy Fathers and Doctors of the Church. Our AI Assistant is programmed with rigorous constraints to respond only based on the Catechism (CCC), Scripture, and Papal Magisterium.
About.pillar3Title: Pillar III: Fraternal Communion (The Prayer Wall)
About.pillar3Desc: Living out the Commandment of Love through mutual intercession. On our Prayer Wall, there are no debates, comments, or public judgment: the community's only response to a brother's request is the sacred, charitable silence of '🙏 I prayed for you'.
About.pillar4Title: Pillar IV: From Screen to Altar (Parish Life)
About.pillar4Desc: The summit of our platform. All biblical meditation and daily prayer flow into concrete sacramental life. Evangelizae encourages and tracks your parish connection and regular attendance at the sacraments of Confession and Eucharist.
About.pillarsSub: Spiritual foundations that govern every line of code and decision in this project
About.c1Desc: In strict adherence to the Deposit of Faith (Depositum Fidei). All reflections and our Theological AI Assistant are grounded strictly in Sacred Scripture, the Catechism (CCC), and Vatican Documents, without private speculation.
About.c2Desc: We intentionally abolished leaderboards, public rankings, and display badges. Your spiritual streak and Rosary consistency are a sacred secret between your soul, God, and His Holy Church.
About.c3Desc: On the Intercessory Wall, we do not allow debate comments or public judgment. When a brother or sister shares a sorrow or intention, the community's only response is silent charity: '🙏 I prayed for you'.
About.c4Desc: We will never charge fees, never sell 'Pro/Premium' subscriptions, never display commercial advertisements, and never put paywalls on the Word of God. Our support comes purely through divine providence and voluntary donations.
About.ctaText: This platform is consecrated to the Immaculate Heart of Mary under the patronage of St. Joseph. Join us on this journey to sanctify daily routine.

Sanctuary.title: Daily Sanctuary
Sanctuary.subtitle: A sacred refuge for your daily prayer and formation
Sanctuary.saintOfDayName: Sts. Louis and Zélie Martin (Parents of St. Thérèse)
Sanctuary.saintOfDayQuote: "The good God gave me a father and a mother more worthy of Heaven than of earth."
Sanctuary.rosaryCardSubtitle: Glorious Mysteries — Contemplation of the Glory of Christ and Our Lady
Sanctuary.gospelCardSnippet: "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart... and your neighbor as yourself." And Jesus said to him: "Go and do likewise."
Sanctuary.planCardDesc: Consecrate your first waking thought to the Holy Trinity and Our Lady.
Sanctuary.communityCardSubtitle: Souls united in silent and fraternal prayer

Rosary.subtitle: Interactive step-by-step guide in strict fidelity to St. Louis de Montfort
Rosary.stepSignOfCross: 1. Sign of the Cross & Montfort Offering
Rosary.stepOurFatherIntentions: 3. Our Father for the Intentions of the Holy Father
Rosary.stepThreeHailMarys: 4. Three Hail Marys (Faith, Hope, and Charity)
Rosary.completedBanner: ✝️ Rosary Completed with Devotion! May Our Lady intercede for your intentions.

Liturgy.title: Daily Liturgy & Word of God
Liturgy.subtitle: Lectio Divina and reverent preparation for Holy Mass
Liturgy.reflectionContent: As the Catechism of the Catholic Church reminds us (CCC 1825), charity upholds and purifies our human ability to love. The parable of the Good Samaritan reveals that our neighbor is anyone who stands in need of compassion and God's sanctifying grace.

Intentions.title: Fraternal Intercessory Wall
Intentions.subtitle: Request prayers or pray for a brother or sister in sacred silence (No debate comments)
Intentions.modalTextLabel: Your Intention (Be concise and trust in God)
Intentions.modalTextPlaceholder: E.g., For the healing and health recovery of my father Anthony...
Intentions.categoryDeceased: For the Deceased (Holy Souls in Purgatory)
Intentions.silentRuleDesc: To preserve fraternal peace and prevent debates or personal judgments, this wall functions exclusively by intercessory prayer (`{prayedForYou}`).

Ai.title: Magisterium Assistant
Ai.subtitle: Secure theological answers grounded strictly in Holy Scripture, Catechism, and Vatican Documents
Ai.inputPlaceholder: Ask your doctrinal, biblical, or liturgical question...
Ai.guardrailWarning: ⚠️ Magisterial Notice: If an inquiry lacks direct grounding in official Church documents indexed within our system, our AI refrains from private speculation and advises consulting a parish priest.
Ai.initialGreeting: Peace and good! I am the Magisterial Assistant of Evangelizae. My mission is to answer your theological, biblical, and liturgical questions with absolute fidelity to Sacred Scripture, the Catechism of the Catholic Church (CCC), and official Vatican documents. How can I assist you in your doctrinal formation today?
Ai.q1Question: Why do Catholics venerate the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Saints?
Ai.q1Ans:
The Catholic Church clearly teaches the theological distinction between Latria (adoration due solely and exclusively to God), Dulia (veneration to saints for having attained heavenly glory by divine grace), and Hyperdulia (special veneration to the Blessed Virgin Mary as the Mother of God - Theotokos).

As the Catechism of the Catholic Church teaches in paragraph 971: "All generations will call me blessed" (Lk 1:48). The Church's devotion to the Blessed Virgin is intrinsic to Christian worship.
Ai.q2Question: What does the Church teach regarding the Real Presence of Christ in the Holy Eucharist?
Ai.q2Ans:
The Catholic Church professes as a dogma of faith that the Holy Eucharist contains truly, really, and substantially the Body and Blood, together with the soul and divinity of Our Lord Jesus Christ (Transubstantiation).

The Catechism (CCC 1374) states: "The mode of Christ's presence under the Eucharistic species is unique. It raises the Eucharist above all the sacraments as the perfection of the spiritual life and the end to which all the sacraments tend."
Ai.q3Question: How should I prepare for a good and holy Confession?
Ai.faqSubtitle: Click on any doctrinal question below to immediately review the grounded response:
Ai.aboutAiText: Our RAG engine is strictly configured without private speculations. It does not offer opinions, but reverently proclaims the immutable truth entrusted to the Holy Church.

Profile.parishPlaceholder: E.g., St. Mary's Cathedral Parish - Chicago, IL

Landing.heroTitle: Freely You Have Received; Freely Give (Mt 10:8)
Landing.heroSubtitle: Evangelizae is a Christian open-source digital sanctuary voluntarily maintained to strengthen your prayer, doctrinal formation, and parish communion. Non-profit, ad-free, and void of digital vanity.
Landing.pillarsSubtitle: A structured apostolate designed to bring your soul closer to Sacred Tradition and the Eucharist
Landing.pillarPrayerDesc: The Holy Rosary meditated with reverence, sensory audio-vibration, and biblical passages for each mystery, restoring inner peace.
Landing.pillarFormationDesc: Daily Liturgy with Church Fathers and doctrinal guidance grounded in Sacred Scripture and the Catechism (CCC).
Landing.pillarCommunionDesc: The Prayer Wall of mutual intercession. No public debates or judgments, just the silent charity of praying for one another.
Landing.pillarChurchTitle: Pillar IV: From Screen to Altar
Landing.pillarChurchDesc: True Christian life happens in the physical Church. Our ultimate purpose is to guide you to your Parish and Eucharistic Adoration.
Landing.philSubtitle: The Word of God has no price tag. Our infrastructure is public, auditable, and collaborative
Landing.philNoAddictionDesc: We do not use retention algorithms. The less screen time you need to pray and seek God, the more we fulfill our mission.
Landing.philNoRankingDesc: We reject leaderboards and comparisons. Your prayer is a private dialogue with Our Lord Jesus Christ and His Holy Church.
Landing.philMagisteriumDesc: All content, liturgy, and magisterial answers strictly follow the teachings of the Roman Catholic Church.
Landing.philFreeForeverDesc: 100% open-source code under a free license on GitHub. Built collaboratively by volunteer Catholic programmers and theologians.
Landing.crisisTitle: Why Do We Choose Not to Commercialize the Sacred?
Landing.crisisSubtitle: We believe that the spiritual nourishment of the Church should never be restricted by paywalls, subscriptions, or commercial ads. Evangelizae is a missionary offering.
Landing.crisisCard1Title: The Bread of Life is Free
Landing.crisisCard1Desc: In the words of Jesus to the Apostles: 'Freely you have received; freely give' (Mt 10:8). Traditional prayers, the Rosary, and the Catechism belong to the spiritual heritage of all faithful without commercial barriers.
Landing.crisisCard2Desc: By opening our source code to the global Catholic community, we ensure that Evangelizae remains pure, free from tracking, void of intrusive ads, and faithful to the Christian mission across generations.
Landing.dailySanctuarySubtitle: A continuous life of prayer to sanctify your day, from the first light of morning to the quiet stillness of night
Landing.sanctuaryMorningDesc: Consecrate the start of your day with the Gospel, canonical readings, and the wisdom of the Holy Doctors of the Church.
Landing.sanctuaryNoonDesc: Pause for the meditated Holy Rosary. With sensory audio and biblical contemplation, elevating the soul amidst daily duties.
Landing.sanctuaryEveningDesc: Theological deepening and doctrinal consultation with absolute fidelity to Conciliar and Catechism documents.
Landing.sanctuaryNightDesc: Quiet recollection on the fraternal Prayer Wall and serene self-examination before God, preparing the heart for rest in divine grace.
Landing.missionDeepTitle: An Apostolate Built By the Community
Landing.missionDeepSubtitle: You too are part of this mission. Learn how to collaborate or intercede for this project
Landing.missionDeepText1: Evangelizae is an open-source project born from the desire to place modern technology at the service of the sanctification of souls. Developers, designers, and catechists donate their time and talent as an act of love for the Church.
Landing.missionDeepText2: If you are a programmer, translator, or theologian, we invite you to contribute to our GitHub repository. And if you do not write code, your greatest contribution is praying for this work and sharing it with your parish brothers and sisters.
Landing.continueSubtitle: No mandatory registration, no commercial data harvesting. Enter in peace to pray or collaborate with our mission.
